data.title: Anthony Scaramucci Heard Hal Finney & Winklevoss Twins, Yet Couldn't 'Care Less' About Bitcoin—Until A White House Moment Changed Everything
data.description:
Scaramucci Couldn’t Care Less About BTC At A Time

On a podcast with Liz Thomas that aired April 15, Scaramucci revealed that he was introduced to Bitcoin back in 2011 while attending a presentation by Hal Finney, the late software developer who received the first Bitcoin transaction from Satoshi Nakamoto.

“I did not understand it,” Scaramucci added. The skepticism stuck around for a while. Fast forward to 2013, Scaramucci took to X, then Twitter, to declare that he didn't know what a Bitcoin was, and couldn't care less.

Scaramucci then recalled that he was reintroduced to Bitcoin during a conference in Las Vegas, where the Winklevoss twins, Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss, came over and began telling him about the leading cryptocurrency.

But Scaramucci was still doubtful.

“My eyes were glazing over, and I was like, ‘Okay, I don’t understand it. I’m not going to invest in it,'” he said.

The Turning Point

But the eureka moment, as he has previously described, came during his brief 11-day stint in 2017 during President Donald Trump's first term.

Scaramucci recalled that then-Treasury Secretary Steve Mnuchin and other officials from the Federal Reserve had come to discuss Bitcoin and blockchain technology at the Oval Office.

“They were talking about a white paper related to potentially digitizing the U.S. dollar. And I was looking at them, and my eyes were not glazed over,” he said. The officials told him that the idea was to digitize the dollar on a blockchain such as Bitcoin.

While he was fired a few days later, he became fascinated by Bitcoin and began extensive research into the cryptocurrency. He soon began investing in Bitcoin via his hedge fund, Skybridge Capital.

From Skeptic To Believer

Today. Scaramucci’s conviction is so strong that he even penned a book about Bitcoin called "The Little Book of Bitcoin,”  a guide to understanding the financial implications of digital asset technology.

Scaramucci has revealed that over 70% of his net worth is invested in Bitcoin, and has set a $1 million price target for the asset by 2032.

Price Action: At the time of writing, BTC was exchanging hands at $73,468.28, down 31.68% in the last 24 hours, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Photo Courtesy: Image via Shutterstock/ Al Teich

data.title: US, UK, And Australia Launch Advanced Undersea Drone Project
data.description:
The United States, United Kingdom and Australia announced a joint effort to develop advanced underwater drone technology designed to safeguard critical seabed infrastructure and strengthen military capabilities across multiple regions.

The initiative marks the first major project under AUKUS Pillar Two, which focuses on advanced defense technologies.

The three allies expect the new system to reach operational readiness by next year, BBC reports.

Project Focus

The program centers on uncrewed undersea vehicles equipped with sophisticated payloads, sensors and weapons systems.

Officials said the drones will support surveillance, reconnaissance, logistics missions and precision strike operations.

John Healey, the UK defense secretary, said Britain will invest £150 million, or about $201 million, into the effort.

Healey acknowledged criticism surrounding the alliance’s pace of execution.

"For too long in Aukus, we talked too much and delivered too little," Healey said. He added, "that has now changed under our three governments".

Infrastructure Security

Defense officials said the technology will help secure underwater assets that support global communications and energy networks. Healey noted that daily life depends heavily on subsea cables and pipelines.

The announcement follows growing concerns over incidents involving damaged seabed infrastructure in Europe and Asia.

Western governments have increasingly raised alarms about suspicious maritime activity near critical underwater networks.

Strategic Context

AUKUS launched in 2021 with plans to deepen military cooperation among the three nations.

The partnership includes nuclear-powered submarine development and collaboration on emerging technologies.

Many analysts view the alliance as a response to expanding Chinese maritime influence across the Indo-Pacific region. Officials, however, declined to directly link the new drone initiative to Russian or Chinese activities, BBC adds.

Submarine Program Continues

The ministers also defended progress under AUKUS Pillar One, which focuses on nuclear-powered submarines. The vessels remain scheduled for deployment during the 2040s.

Richard Marles, Australia’s defense minister, said work continues on infrastructure projects supporting future submarine operations.

He said HMAS Stirling in Western Australia should be prepared to host rotational submarine deployments by late 2027.

data.title: Nvidia Stock At Key Support Ahead of Big Windows PC Launch
data.description:
Microsoft to Launch First Nvidia-Powered Windows PC

Nvidia and Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) stocks will be in the spotlight next week as the latter launches the first Windows PC powered by Nvidia chips. 

According to Axios, the two companies will unveil their joint projects at the Computex trade show in Taiwan and Microsoft's Build conference in San Francisco. 

NVIDIA aims that the CPU business will complement its GPU business, which dominates the AI industry. Its most recent results showed that its revenue surged 85% in the first quarter to over $81 billion. 

The company's guidance is that its revenue will then grow to $91 billion in the current quarter, a figure that may be conservative. For one, the estimate ignored any sales to China as the US has already allowed ten Chinese companies to buy these chips. 

Nvidia Stock Price Has Formed a Break-and-Retest Pattern

The daily chart shows that the Nvidia stock price may be preparing a strong comeback. That's because it has retested the important support level at $212, which was the previous all-time high. 

It was also the upper side of the cup-and-handle pattern, a common continuation sign. A break-and-retest normally confirms a breakout.

NVDA stock chart | Source: TradingView

This C&H pattern had a depth of about 22%. Extrapolating this percentage from its upper side gives it a target of $260. This view will be confirmed if it moves above the all-time high of $236. 

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Husband, 42, Demanded Separate Finances To Protect His Kid But Now Expects Wife To Help With $65K A Year School — 'Technically Her Stepdaughter'
data.description:
Marriage is supposed to merge hearts, lives, and occasionally streaming passwords. Bank accounts? That part gets trickier when second marriages, teenagers, and a $65,000-a-year college acceptance letter enter the chat.

The setup sounded clean enough on paper. The couple shared a joint account for household expenses while everything else stayed separate. Then college acceptance season arrived with the financial equivalent of a flying piano.

Don't Miss:

A $65K-A-Year Reality Check

The husband said his daughter was accepted into a school costing roughly $65,000 per year. Her college savings account contains about $80,000, which he admitted would only cover around a year and a half.

That's when the arrangement he originally pushed for suddenly started looking less appealing.

"Last week I asked my wife if she'd be willing to help cover some of the costs since we're married and my daughter is technically her stepdaughter now," he wrote.

His wife declined, reminding him the entire reason they structured finances separately was because he wanted to protect his daughter's money in the first place.

The husband said he became upset and told her, "I didn't think she'd actually hold me to that when it comes to my daughter's education."

He also argued that "this is different than regular expenses" and that family should help family.

Reddit readers, however, were not exactly rushing to pass the collection plate.

One commenter boiled the situation down into a single brutal sentence:

"What's mine is mine. And what's yours is also mine."

Another user pointed out the contradiction at the center of the dispute.

"You can't change the rules when it benefits you and your daughter."

Trending: Make your first trade—or your next one—with commission-free investing and up to $1,000 in stock from SoFi.

Several commenters also questioned why the daughter's biological mother was criticizing the new wife instead of contributing more herself. Others noted the daughter still has options that do not involve a $260,000 undergraduate bill.

"Your daughter can go to a cheaper school," one commenter wrote bluntly.

The "Technically Her Stepdaughter" Debate

The phrase that drew some of the strongest reactions was the husband describing his daughter as "technically her stepdaughter now."

For many readers, that wording made the situation feel less like a shared parenting discussion and more like a legal technicality being deployed after the budget spreadsheet caught fire.

The timeline also mattered. The wife became a stepmother only two years ago, when the daughter was already in high school. Reddit users repeatedly argued that helping raise a child for more than a decade is very different from suddenly inheriting part of a four-year college bill near graduation.

One commenter told him:

"The exact thing you said you wanted to protect yourself from is what you are expecting your wife to do for you now."

Others focused less on the money and more on the fallout inside the marriage. The husband admitted his ex-wife somehow learned about the disagreement and called his current wife "selfish." Meanwhile, his daughter has reportedly become "really cold" toward her stepmother.

That detail sparked another wave of criticism, with many readers arguing the private disagreement should never have spilled into the wider family dynamic.

Separate Finances Can Get Messy Fast

Separate finances in second marriages are hardly unusual, especially when children from previous relationships are involved. Couples often divide household costs while keeping retirement savings, inheritance plans, or college money independent.

The trouble usually starts when emotional expectations quietly drift away from the original agreement.

Because once a marriage reaches the point where one spouse says "we agreed to keep things separate" and the other says "this is different," the debate usually is not about money anymore.

data.title: Charles Hoskinson's Cardano Has Made Just $352,000 In 2026 Despite $8.2 Billion Market Cap
data.description:
Cardano Fees Have Plunged This Year

Despite having an $8.2 billion market capitalization, Cardano has made just $352,000 this year, data by DeFi Llama shows. It made just $238,000 in the first quarter, its lowest level since the fourth quarter of 2020. At its peak, the network generated $4.2 million in fees. 

Cardano's decentralized finance ecosystem has continued to worsen, with the total value locked dropping to just $128 million. Ethereum, in contrast, has over $43 billion in assets. Similarly, as the chart below shows, the volume handled by decentralized exchange networks in Cardano dropped to $222 million in Q1 from $408 million in Q4. 

More users are abandoning Cardano, with the number of active addresses falling to 1.6 million in Q1, down from 15.1 million in Q4'21. 

ADA Price is Nearing Record Low

The ongoing Cardano woes likely explain why ADA, its token, has crashed and is hovering near its all-time low. It has dropped to $0.2353, a few points above its all-time low of $0.2073. This price is much lower than its all-time high of $3 when its market cap was over $90 billion.

ADA has continued to underperform its closest peers. For example, it has crashed by 65% in the last 12 months, while HYPE has jumped by 86% in the same period. Solana and XRP prices have dropped by 47%, 40%, while BNB has risen by over 2.5%.

Image: Shutterstock
